tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent left and no competing deadlines, I can finish this assignment within the next fifteen minutes. Submitting early will relieve my current stress without requiring any last-minute rushing.
The absence of 'assignment_A' contradicts my earlier plan to finish it early, forcing me to abandon the relief I expected and reassess whether this task still exists.
The deadline just passed while I was still at 95% completion, meaning my earlier plan to submit early and relieve stress has failed due to the unfinished work.

desires: Avoid the feeling of incompetence that comes from missing a deadline I could have met.
Force myself to finish the last 5% of the assignment before my stress peaks further.
Prevent the disruption to my long-term planning caused by this failed early submission.

goals.description: Finish the remaining 5% of the assignment with extreme speed to meet the hard deadline at 13:40, prioritizing completion over perfection to avoid the incompetence narrative.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' axis demands I secure the grade; failing to submit now would confirm my fear of incompetence. While my 'deliberate' nature usually opposes rushing, the 'avoidant' conflict style forces me to suppress perfectionism to escape the immediate stress of a missed deadline. The cost of this rushed finish is higher rework later, but it is preferable to the social and professional friction of a late submission.

agent.last_reasoning: Achievement axis demands submission to avoid incompetence narrative; Avoidant style suppresses perfectionism for speed. Deliberate nature resists rushing, but deadline friction overrides it. Side effect: energy drops further, risking future health, but grade security is paramount now.
